text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint starts with a planning meeting where the team selects tasks from the backlog, a prioritized list of work. They break tasks into user stories, which describe features from a user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and obstacles. After each sprint, they review the work and adjust plans. This iterative development process allows teams to adapt quickly and deliver value regularly.
